channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Itália derrotada: adeus, sonhos felizes do passado
item.description: Italy's national football team was eliminated in a penalty shootout by Bosnia in the European playoff for the 2026 World Cup, marking their third consecutive absence from the tournament — a negative record among former champions. The loss was portrayed as both dramatic and moving, with coach Gennaro Gattuso and fans expressing resigned disappointment after years of failure. Italy's absence leaves only Germany among the 48 qualified teams with a chance to match Brazil's five World Cup titles.

item.title: Por que a geração Z não faz o ‘L’ e como isso pode atrapalhar o projeto de reeleição de Lula
item.description: Despite historically strong youth support, Lula's voting intention among 16- to 24-year-olds dropped from 62% in 2022 to 43% in 2024, according to Datafolha. This decline persists despite youth-focused initiatives like Pé-de-Meia, Projovem, and the National Youth Observatory, as 73% of young voters now disapprove of his government, per AtlasIntel, due to weak emotional ties to PT's past achievements and perceptions of economic stagnation.

item.title: “Vou apoiar Lula independentemente das decisões do meu partido”, diz André Fufuca
item.description: André Fufuca, former Minister of Sports, states he will support Lula regardless of his party's decisions, after being removed from national leadership and losing state command for refusing to leave the government. He praises Lula as Brazil's best president, citing achievements such as reduced inflation and unemployment, social CNH, and income tax exemption for those earning up to R$5,000, and says he would sign a CPI request to investigate the Master scandal. Despite Lula's high rejection, Fufuca believes his re-election is the best path for Brazil.

item.title: Desalento
item.description: Brazilian voters show widespread disillusionment, with six in ten expressing despair and fear about the future, according to Datafolha. Despite Lula’s welfare programs lifting nearly 12 million people out of extreme poverty, his disapproval remains high and rising, matching that of opponents like Jair and Flávio Bolsonaro. The government is responding by manufacturing its own narratives, targeting businessmen as scapegoats for fuel prices, and leveraging anti-U.S. rhetoric to rally support, while the opposition remains fragmented and unable to present a cohesive alternative.

item.title: SobeDesce: de Alckmin a Eduardo Leite
item.description: Vice President Geraldo Alckmin was confirmed as Lula’s running mate for the 2026 re-election campaign. Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite faced criticism for complaining about being sidelined by the PSD for a presidential bid, echoing similar behavior from 2021 when he lost the PSDB primaries.

item.title: Saiba quais são os próximos passos de Messias até o STF
item.description: President Lula has submitted the nomination of Jorge Messias, minister of the AGU, to fill the STF vacancy left by Luís Roberto Barroso. The nominee will undergo a hearing in the CCJ and, if approved, proceed to the Senate plenary, where he needs at least 41 votes. Although Messias has declared support from 25 senators and 10 of the 14 required in the CCJ, he lacks a confirmed majority, though Senate rejections of STF nominees are extremely rare.

item.title: 2.639 cidades têm mais Bolsa Família que emprego com carteira
item.description: In 2026, 2,639 Brazilian municipalities have more families receiving Bolsa Família than people with formal employment, a 9.5% decrease from 2023. The situation is most severe in the Northeast and North, where 8 of the 9 municipalities with zero formal job registrations are located. Nationally, there are 38.6 Bolsa Família beneficiaries for every 100 formal work contracts, indicating reduced reliance on the program after its peak in January 2023 under Lula’s administration.

item.title: Moraes e Toffoli foram alvos de 12 pedidos de impeachment após Master
item.description: Supreme Court ministers Alexandre de Moraes and Dias Toffoli were targeted with 12 new impeachment requests (6 each) following developments in the Banco Master case, bringing the total of pending actions in the Senate to 102. Moraes accounts for nearly half of all impeachment requests (50), tied to his wife’s contract with the bank, while Toffoli faces allegations over asset sales linked to the same financial group.

item.title: 9 Estados têm mais Bolsa Família que trabalho com carteira assinada
item.description: As of February 2026, nine Brazilian states have more families receiving Bolsa Família than formally employed workers with signed work cards: Maranhão, Pará, Piauí, Bahia, Paraíba, Amazonas, Alagoas, Acre, and Amapá. Despite this, dependency on the program declined from 49.6 beneficiaries per 100 formal workers in January 2023 to 38.6 in February 2026, driven by formal job growth and a benefit eligibility review that removed 2.1 million families in 2025. The downward trend has stabilized in early 2026 after falling from 13 to nine such states since 2023.

item.title: O superteto do Supremo
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court (STF) unanimously ruled that the constitutional remuneration cap for public servants does not apply to judges and prosecutors until Congress legislates, concluding judgments on so-called 'penduricalhos'. The decision increases transparency by standardizing over 3,000 previously obscured benefits, defines which allowances may exceed the cap, and aims to curb creative remuneration practices in the judiciary. The fiscal impact is substantial: the Judiciary and the Public Prosecutor's Office spent R$ 9.8 billion and R$ 7.2 billion, respectively, on extra-cap benefits, according to an STF technical commission.

item.title: Eleitor atolado em dívidas é problema para Lula
item.description: The explosive rise in Brazilian household debt, driven by aggressive lending practices with interest rates well above the Selic, poses a significant challenge for Lula’s government. This situation increases financial strain on families and may negatively affect public perception of economic management.

item.title: Inelegibilidade de Castro abre corrida no PL por segunda vaga ao Senado
item.description: The ineligibility of former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL), ruled by the TSE, has triggered a scramble within the party for the second Senate seat in the 2026 elections. Right-wing politicians are positioning themselves to fill the vacancy left by his candidacy ban.

item.title: Troca-troca partidário dos deputados desidrata União Brasil e infla PL
item.description: The 2026 party window has already moved 73 federal deputies, about 14% of the Chamber, with União Brasil suffering the largest losses and PL being the main destination for those who switched. This reorganization of forces reflects ongoing electoral strategies aimed at strengthening bases for the 2026 election race.

item.title: Candidatura de Caiado impacta estratégia de Raquel Lyra em PE e dá munição a João Campos
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado's presidential candidacy for the PSD complicates Raquel Lyra's strategy, which aimed to distance herself from opposition to Lula after switching from PSDB to PSD. The party's right-wing, Bolsonarist alignment gives ammunition to her rival João Campos and enables the opposition to link her image to the PSD's national rhetoric, despite her efforts to attract center-left allies like Túlio Gadêlha and emphasize regional symbols to shield herself politically.

item.title: Escala 6x1: governo quer celeridade à votação do projeto e defende limitar jornada a 40 horas semanais
item.description: The Lula government seeks to fast-track a bill ending the 6x1 work schedule, proposing a 40-hour weekly workweek with two days off and no salary reduction, to be processed under constitutional urgency. The initiative aims to turn the issue into a political flagship ahead of the 2026 elections, despite resistance from the Chamber, which prefers discussing it via a Constitutional Amendment Proposal (PEC). Chamber President Hugo Motta had already submitted a PEC on the matter in February, but it has not progressed since.

item.title: Governo se mobiliza para evitar mudanças à MP da tabela do frete para caminhoneiros no Congresso
item.description: The Lula government is mobilizing congressional support to block amendments that would substantially alter a provisional measure expanding the ANTT's authority to enforce and penalize violations of the minimum freight table. The measure, issued last month, aimed to ease tensions with truckers by strengthening enforcement of floor prices in road freight transport. The initiative seeks to preserve the recent agreement with the sector, preventing setbacks that could reignite conflict.

item.title: Sucessão de Gleisi na articulação política segue indefinida e sob pressão de garantir aprovação de Messias no STF
item.description: President Lula has not yet decided who will replace Minister Gleisi Hoffmann as head of the Secretariat of Institutional Relations (SRI), as she must step down by this Saturday to run for Senate in Paraná. The new minister must have strong congressional ties, be a trusted confidant of Lula, and help secure approval for Jorge Messias’ nomination to the STF, which faces opposition in the Senate, particularly from Senate President Davi Alcolumbre. Names such as Otto Alencar, José Guimarães, and Wellington Dias have been discussed, but no decision has been made due to the challenge of finding a suitable politician not running in the 2026 elections.

item.title: Balanço ambiental: Marina Silva deixa ministério com queda no desmatamento, mas tem gestão marcada por reveses políticos
item.description: Marina Silva exits the Ministry of Environment with a 58% reduction in Amazon deforestation and 32% in the Cerrado between 2022 and 2025, having launched the National Climate Plan and the Climate Fund (R$ 52.4 billion), created 13 new Conservation Units, and strengthened environmental governance after the Bolsonaro era, despite political setbacks such as the congressional approval of the new Environmental Licensing law.

item.title: STF pauta eleições no Rio, Ferrogrão e Lei Ferrari em abril
item.description: On April 8, the STF will rule on three nationally significant issues: the electoral process for Rio de Janeiro's governorship following Claudio Castro's resignation, the lawsuit challenging the reduction of Jamanxim National Park for the Ferrogrão railway, and the resumption of analysis on the Ferrari Law governing automaker-dealer relations. These cases involve federalism, environmental protection, and economic regulation, with direct implications for the 2026 elections.

item.title: Bolsonaros são patriotas de vermelho, azul e branco
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro delivered a speech at CPAC, attacking globalist elites, environmentalism, the woke agenda, and the deep state, while protesting his father Jair Bolsonaro's imprisonment. He accused President Lula of aligning with China and proposed aligning Brazil with the United States if elected. The content is exclusive to subscribers.

item.title: Eleições 2026: janela partidária termina nesta sexta-feira; saiba o que está em jogo
item.description: The partisan window for the 2026 elections ends this Friday (3), allowing federal, state, and district deputies to switch parties without risking their mandates, as the rule applies only to the proportional system. Outside this period, party changes are permitted only in specific cases, such as significant program changes or serious political discrimination. The closing of the window triggered intense negotiations in the Chamber of Deputies.

item.title: Por que distribuidoras de combustíveis resistem às medidas do governo para conter a alta dos preços?
item.description: Major fuel distributors in Brazil, including Vibra (operating BR and Ipiranga) and Raízen (Shell), are resisting the federal government's diesel subsidy program despite efforts to curb prices driven by the Middle East war. The government offers R$0.32 per liter to producers and importers, with a second proposal of R$1.20 per liter to be shared with states, but companies cite concerns over margins and price controls. Their refusal risks weakening the relief package, fueling inflation, and increasing the likelihood of trucker protests, prompting the economic team to consider adjustments and the ANP to open a public consultation.

item.title: Após crítica de Lula, Petrobras estuda cancelar leilão de gás de cozinha
item.description: Petrobras is studying the cancellation of its March 31 LPG (cooking gas) auction following criticism from President Lula, who labeled the auction 'banditry' and stated it occurred against the will of the government and Petrobras leadership. The auction saw bids reach up to 117% above refinery value, raising concerns about consumer price increases. The Ministry of Mines and Energy has requested Senacon to evaluate potential abusive practices in the domestic LPG market.

item.title: Lula rebate críticas dos EUA ao Pix: ‘Ninguém vai nos fazer mudar’
item.description: President Lula defended Pix as a Brazilian asset, stating the government will not change the system under U.S. pressure, despite a USTR report labeling Pix and the 'blusinhas' tax as protectionist practices. He emphasized the goal is to improve the tool to better serve the population. In the same event, Lula criticized Donald Trump for closing the Strait of Hormuz, linking the move to rising fuel and food prices in Brazil.

item.title: Bolsonaro prioriza maioria pró-impeachment do STF no Senado enquanto Flávio evita expor embate
item.description: Bolsonaro is focused on securing a pro-impeachment majority of STF ministers in the Senate by 2027, backing right-wing candidates for the October elections, even as his son Flávio adopts a more moderate tone in the presidential race. This strategy aims to strengthen the bolsonarist base in Congress to pressure the Judiciary, contrasting with Flávio’s more institutional approach. The move reflects an effort to institutionalize opposition to the STF through legislative control.

item.title: Fachin critica relatório dos EUA e cita "distorções" sobre decisões do STF. Vídeo
item.description: Supreme Court President Justice Edson Fachin criticized a U.S. House Judiciary Committee report that questioned Brazilian Supreme Court decisions on content removal in social media, calling the document 'distorted' in its characterization of the rulings and defending them as necessary to combat digital crimes such as attempted coup and criminal association. He emphasized that freedom of expression is not absolute and cannot be invoked to justify crimes defined by law.

item.title: Eduardo Bolsonaro diz que não apoiar Flávio favorece Lula
item.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro claims that not supporting Flávio Bolsonaro's candidacy favors Lula and urges voters to reject candidates who do not publicly endorse the senator, including those from the PL. He emphasizes that voters should communicate their reasoning directly to candidates. The statement comes amid a tightly contested race, with recent polls showing a technical tie between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro.

item.title: Governo oficializa exonerações de Rui Costa, Márcio França e Jader Filho
item.description: The federal government officially announced the dismissals of Rui Costa (Chief of Staff), Jader Filho (Ministry of Cities), Márcio França (Ministry of Entrepreneurship), and Irajá Lacerda (executive secretary of the Ministry of Agriculture), as published in an extra edition of the Official Gazette on April 2. The departures are due to the electoral incompatibility deadline ending on April 4, which requires public officials to leave office before running for election to prevent misuse of state resources. Rui Costa will step down to run for the Senate from Bahia.

item.title: Fachin rebate EUA e diz que STF garante liberdade de expressão
item.description: Supreme Federal Court President Edson Fachin rebutted a U.S. House Judiciary Committee report alleging freedom of expression violations in Brazil, calling its characterizations distorted and affirming the STF's role in protecting this right under the Constitution. He cited rulings prohibiting censorship in universities during the 2018 elections and opposing judicial harassment of journalists, while emphasizing that freedom of expression is not absolute and may be limited in cases of crime or conflict with other fundamental rights. Fachin stated Brazil will provide diplomatic clarifications and defended content removal on social media when tied to investigations of criminal platform use, particularly threats to democracy.

item.title: Novo condiciona pré-candidaturas ao Senado a apoio a impeachment de ministros do STF
item.description: Eduardo Ribeiro, president of the Novo Party, stated that Senate pre-candidates from the party must publicly declare support for the impeachment of STF ministers. He said this is a requirement, not just an asset, for anyone seeking to run under the Novo banner. The comment was made in an interview with Papo Antagonista.

item.title: Fachin diz que relatório dos EUA faz distorção de decisões do STF sobre liberdade de expressão
item.description: Supreme Court President Edson Fachin responded to a U.S. House Judiciary Committee report that accused Brazil’s judiciary of censoring freedom of expression, particularly through decisions by Minister Alexandre de Moraes targeting the Bolsonaro family. Fachin stated that freedom of expression is a fundamental right in Brazil but may be exceptionally limited to prevent crimes, such as those committed by digital militias seeking coups, and denied any political censorship. He also noted that Brazil’s platform accountability model is not unique and has parallels in the United States.

item.title: De Gracyanne a Edmundo: saiba quais famosos se filiaram a partidos
item.description: Brazilian celebrities are affiliating with political parties ahead of the 2026 elections, primarily aiming for seats in the Chamber of Deputies. Notable names include Gracyanne Barbosa and Val Marchiori (Republicans), Silvia Abravanel (PSD), and Antonia Fontenelle (PSDB), all in articulation stages but without formal candidacies yet. Parties seek to leverage the public visibility of these figures to expand their electoral reach.

item.title: Com governo em baixa entre mulheres, Janja faz vídeo sobre luta feminina
item.description: First Lady Janja released a video supporting women's rights amid declining government approval among women, with 58% disapproval according to a PoderData survey. She stated she will continue working with determination and affection to win the battles for women.

item.title: Lula posa de defensor do Pix após receber instruções de marqueteiro do governo
item.description: President Lula received guidance from the government's Communications Minister, Sidônio Palmeira, to present himself as a defender of the Pix payment system during an official agenda in Bahia. The recommendation came before his public statement on Pix, amid international scrutiny, including a White House report highlighting potential risks associated with the system.

item.title: Fachin reage a relatório dos EUA que acusou Moraes de censura e defende decisões do STF
item.description: Supreme Court President Edson Fachin defended the court's rulings on social media content, rejecting a U.S. House Judiciary Committee report that accused Alexandre de Moraes of censorship and lawfare. He stated the measures aim to prevent the criminal instrumentalization of platforms by digital militias and that freedom of expression may face limited restrictions when necessary to protect other fundamental rights or punish crimes defined by law.

item.title: Governo Lula articula plano para conter alta do querosene de aviação
item.description: The Lula government is discussing measures to mitigate the impact of rising aviation fuel (QAV) costs on airlines following a 56% price increase by Petrobras. The plan, being developed by the Ministry of Ports and Airports (MPor), includes reducing taxes such as PIS/Cofins and IOF, cutting income tax on aircraft leasing, and creating a specific financing line for the sector. The initiative responds to President Lula’s call for urgency, as QAV now accounts for 45% of operational costs for Brazilian airlines.

item.title: Márcio França deixa governo Lula para centrar esforços em eleição em São Paulo
item.description: Entrepreneurship Minister Márcio França (PSB) announced he will leave Lula's government to focus on the São Paulo election and Lula's 2026 re-election bid. The decision followed a meeting with President Lula, and França thanked Vice President Geraldo Alckmin for the trust. He emphasized his experience as São Paulo's vice governor and governor, his commitment to democracy, but did not clarify whether he will run for Senate or indirectly support PSB campaigns in the state.

item.title: França deixa ministério e pode ser vice de Haddad em SP
item.description: Márcio França (PSB-SP) resigned from the Ministry of Entrepreneurship after a meeting with President Lula and may become Fernando Haddad's (PT) running mate for vice-governor of São Paulo in 2026. His exact role in the ticket has not yet been decided, as two positions are open: vice-governor and one of the Senate seats. His departure from the federal government indicates alignment with the PT's strategy for the upcoming state elections.

item.title: Governo tende a ampliar medidas emergenciais com provável expansão da guerra
item.description: The Brazilian government faces mounting pressure from the potential escalation of the war in the Middle East, which could lead to diesel shortages and rising food prices, particularly from May onward if control of the Strait of Hormuz remains contested. Despite economic risks and corruption scandals undermining Lula’s image, it is too early to predict an opposition victory, as the government’s response to the conflict will be decisive — just as in 2022, when Bolsonaro overcame similar challenges. The comparison with the past election serves to avoid premature conclusions about the war’s immediate impact on the 2026 elections.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro chama PT de “especialista em mentir” sobre Pix
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) called the PT a 'specialist in lying' in response to statements by Lula and Lindbergh Farias regarding the Pix payment system, following a U.S. report criticizing Brazil's Pix. He shared an Instagram video where ChatGPT debunked the claim that he would abolish Pix, labeling it as fake news. President Lula reiterated that Pix belongs to Brazil and will not be changed.

item.title: Flávio rebate Lula e nega intenção de acabar com Pix
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) denied in a social media video that he plans to tax the Pix payment system if elected president, calling the claim a 'PT fake news'. He stated that Pix is a legacy of former President Jair Bolsonaro and pushed back against criticisms from Lula, reinforcing his 2026 presidential pre-candidacy.

item.title: Márcio França deixa governo Lula para disputar eleição em São Paulo
item.description: Marcio França (PSB-SP) announced he will resign as Minister of Entrepreneurship to run in the 2026 São Paulo elections, following a meeting with President Lula. He has not yet specified whether he will run as Haddad’s vice-governor or for a Senate seat, with two positions open. His resignation comes two days before the disqualification deadline, and he now competes with Marina Silva (Rede-SP), who also left her ministerial post to run in the election.

item.title: Mulher de Moraes confirma voos em jatinhos de Vorcaro e cita honorários
item.description: Viviane Barci de Moraes, lawyer and wife of Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes, stated she used private jets owned by Daniel Vorcaro, founder of Banco Master, with payments made as compensation for legal fees, according to Folha de S.Paulo. Her law firm confirmed hiring flights from Prime Aviation between May and October 2025 but denied Vorcaro or Fábiano Zettel were ever on board. Moraes’ office firmly denied that the justice ever traveled on any of Vorcaro’s aircraft or in his company.

item.title: Declaração de Lula sobre senadores complica relação pré-sabatina de Messias
item.description: President Lula's comment that senators 'think they are God' has heightened tensions with Congress, particularly Senate President Davi Alcolumbre, ahead of Jorge Messias' STF confirmation hearing. Despite the strained atmosphere, government allies claim Messias already has sufficient votes for approval in the CCJ and plenary. The remark was seen by some as a provocation that could undermine political negotiation.

item.title: PL sela apoio à candidatura de Ciro Gomes no Ceará
item.description: The national PL has decided to support Ciro Gomes’ (PSDB) candidacy for governor of Ceará in 2026, prioritizing the chance to defeat the local PT, despite Michelle Bolsonaro’s opposition. The agreement includes nominating the father of deputy André Fernandes for a Senate seat and Roberto Cláudio as the vice-gubernatorial candidate.

item.title: ‘No tempo de Davi': mensagem bíblica ou prazo para análise do nome de Messias ao STF pelo Senado?
item.description: Columnist Carlos Andreazza criticizes Senate President Davi Alcolumbre for unilaterally controlling the timeline of the analysis of Jorge Messias’ nomination to the STF, likening it to 'Davi’s time' and questioning whether it is a biblical message or merely an act of personal power. He argues that the hearing depends solely on Alcolumbre’s will, which could delay filling a Supreme Court vacancy and affect the judgment of thousands of cases.

item.title: Márcio França pede demissão de ministério para focar na corrida eleitoral em SP
item.description: Minister of Entrepreneurship Márcio França (PSB-SP) resigned to focus on the São Paulo election, following a meeting with President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva. He announced the decision on Thursday, May 2, via social media platform X, stating his intent to return to state politics with leadership and independence.

item.title: 'Apodreça na cadeia': Tarcísio sobre tenente-coronel réu por feminicídio
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos) commented on the retirement of Lieutenant Colonel Geraldo Leite Rosa Neto, who is detained and charged with the femicide of police officer Gisele and procedural fraud, stating that the accused should 'rot in jail'.

item.title: Ministério de Minas e Energia pede apuração de 'possíveis práticas abusivas' em leilão de gás de cozinha da Petrobras
item.description: The Ministry of Mines and Energy requested Senacon to evaluate possible abusive practices in Petrobras’ LPG (cooking gas) auctions, where premiums exceeded 100% above reference prices. The government stated the auction was conducted against the orientation of Petrobras and federal authorities, and President Lula announced it will be annulled to prevent poor consumers from bearing abusive costs, citing war-related fuel price impacts from the Iran conflict.

item.title: “Dizer a verdade faz o dedo de muita gente ‘didireita’ cair”, diz Carluxo
item.description: Former Rio de Janeiro city councilor Carlos Bolsonaro accused members of the right of trying to 'bury alive' former president Jair Bolsonaro and allowing a narrative that harms the presidential candidacy of Senator Flávio Bolsonaro. He said that simply telling the truth in one or two lines makes the finger of many people on the 'far right' fall. The remarks were made in an interview with O Antagonista.
